metadata.name: backend.ai-kernel
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 or later (LGPLv3+)

modules.name: ai.backend.kernel.service
documentation:
Parses and interpolates the service-definition templates stored as json in
`/etc/backend.ai/servce-defs` of Backend.AI containers.

See more details at [the documentation about adding new kernel images](https://docs.backend.ai/en/latest/dev/adding-kernels.html#service-ports).

classes.name: ai.backend.kernel.r_server_ms.Runner
documentation: Implements an adaptor to Microsoft R Server API.

functions.name: ai.backend.kernel.utils.find_executable
documentation: Find the first executable regular file in the given list of paths.

functions.name: ai.backend.kernel.jupyter_client.aexecute_interactive
documentation:
Async version of jupyter_client's execute_interactive method.

https://github.com/jupyter/jupyter_client/blob/9f1c379/jupyter_client/client.py#L415

classes.name: ai.backend.kernel.python.drawing.turtle.Vec2D
documentation: A helper class taken from Python stdlib's Turtle package.

modules.name: ai.backend.kernel.app
documentation:
This is a special kernel runner for application-only containers
which do not provide query/batch-mode code execution.

classes.name: ai.backend.kernel.terminal.Terminal
documentation: A wrapper for a terminal-based app.

functions.name: ai.backend.kernel.compat.asyncio_run_forever

methods.name: ai.backend.kernel.base.BaseRunner._init_jupyter_kernel
documentation:
Detect ipython kernel spec for backend.ai and start it if found.

Called after `init_with_loop`. `jupyter_kspec_name` should be defined to
initialize jupyter kernel.

methods.name: ai.backend.kernel.base.BaseRunner.init_with_loop
documentation: Initialize after the event loop is created.

methods.name: ai.backend.kernel.base.BaseRunner.query
documentation:
Run user's code in query mode.

The default interface is jupyter kernel. To use different interface,
`Runner` subclass should override this method.

methods.name: ai.backend.kernel.base.BaseRunner.complete
documentation:
Return the list of strings to be shown in the auto-complete list.

The default interface is jupyter kernel. To use different interface,
`Runner` subclass should override this method.

methods.name: ai.backend.kernel.base.BaseRunner.interrupt
documentation:
Interrupt the running user code (only called for query-mode).

The default interface is jupyter kernel. To use different interface,
`Runner` subclass should implement its own `complete` method.

methods.name: ai.backend.kernel.base.BaseRunner.run_subproc
documentation: A thin wrapper for an external command.
